source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As we onboard our first design partners, Cogent is launching the world's first AI cyber taskforce, which is composed of AI agents capable of human-caliber reasoning for cybersecurity tasks. We are looking for talented, ambitious AI/ML Engineers who are excited to build in the Applied AI space.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Architect and launch the world’s first AI Cyber Taskforce
   
   - Work closely with our design customers to design and launch our flagship AI agents that are capable of automating tasks in vulnerability management
   
   - Understand business goals, product and technology strategies, and customer requirements and implement business-critical AI systems to achieve them
   
   - Identify appropriate datasets and data representations to support system development
   
   - Decompose existing enterprise security workflows into sub-tasks that can be modeled as an autonomous system
   
   - Experiment with the latest advancements in long-term reasoning and planning to design systems that allows Cogent to evolve the data flywheel of our AI products from initially highly supervised to eventually autonomous and managed by exception

 - Build and extend Cogent’s Gen AI Platform
   
   - Implement reusable system components for ranking, retrieval and search systems powered by LLMs for agentic automation
   
   - Put in place frameworks to train, evaluate, and stress-test ML systems, and extend improvements on Cogent’s Gen AI platform
   
   - Stay on top of and when appropriate, propose incorporating cutting edge developments in ML and Gen AI into Cogent’s AI systems

 - Help create a robust engineering culture at Cogent
   
   - Onboard, support and uplevel future team members
   
   - Mentor and grow future junior team members
   
   - Actively contribute to Engineering Excellence, Operational Excellence and Recruiting initiatives




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Multiple years of relevant experience in developing and deploying AI-driven systems, with a proven track record of architecting practical, production-grade solutions that solve real-world problems

 - Expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and AI models, with a deep understanding of how to apply these technologies to build scalable, performant systems in production environments

 - Experience with designing and deploying agentic systems and autonomous agents, as well as integrating these AI components into larger applications or products

 - Ability to address and navigate real-world scale and performance challenges, ensuring that AI models and systems are efficient, robust, and performant under production constraints

 - Familiarity with cutting-edge AI technologies, including reinforcement learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and larg

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As we work with our first design partners, Cogent is building a suite of backend services and integrations that will power our autonomous AI workers. We are looking for talented, ambitious Backend Senior and Staff Engineers who are excited to build in the Applied AI space.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Design and implement key backend, integration platform subsystems
   
   - Understand business goals, product and technology strategies and customer requirements and implement supporting backend and integration platform subsystems to achieve them
   
   - Adapt and evolve systems and plans to implement newly identified design partner and customer requirements
   
   - Adapt and evolve systems to implement non-functional requirements (compliance, security, enterprise integration etc.)

 - Laying down infrastructure foundations for growth and scalability
   
   - Prepare Cogent for future headcount, customer, and data growth by working with a team to design and build an infrastructure that will scale and that achieves requirements in the areas of product capabilities, non-functional needs (reliability, security, disaster recovery, etc.).

 - Help create a robust engineering culture at Cogent
   
   - Onboard, support and uplevel future team members
   
   - Participate in, provide feedback on, and iterate interview process as we scale the team up
   
   - Actively contribute to Engineering Excellence, Operational Excellence and Recruiting initiatives




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of professional experience as a hands-on engineer and technical leader, overseeing and contributing to multiple backend systems and infrastructure projects

 - Expertise in architecting, developing, and scaling backend systems, including both stateless and stateful architectures, to support high-performance, enterprise-level applications

 - Proven experience with distributed systems, microservices, and serverless architectures, ensuring robustness, scalability, and fault tolerance

 - Strong knowledge of backend programming languages such as Python, Go or similar, with a deep understanding of concurrency, memory management, and performance optimization

 - Experience with API design and implementation (RESTful and/or gRPC), integrating with frontend systems, data platforms, and third-party services

 - Solid experience with containerization, orchestration, and deployment technologies like Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, and CI/CD pipelines in multi-cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure)




FOR CALIFORNIA BASED APPLICANTS

The standard base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $300,000 annually. Compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be

source_jobs.title: Fullstack Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent is building the world's first AI cyber taskforce, which is composed of AI agents capable of human-caliber reasoning for cybersecurity tasks. We are looking for talented, ambitious Senior and Staff Engineers who are excited to build in the Applied AI space.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Own the product. 
   
   - You're a technical founder type who is willing to deep dive and get your hands dirty to build the best product. 
   
   - This also means that you will have a high level of autonomy and responsibility in shaping the technical roadmap and product strategy.

 - Ship, ship, ship. 
   
   - Design and implement Cogent’s AI product experience for enterprises that run based on our multi-tenant cloud and single-tenant enterprise platform.
   
   - The first products that you will be involved in launching are: customer portal, knowledge engine, internal labeling tool, and external customer dashboard.

 - Build the new standard for generative interfaces in cybersecurity. 
   
   - World-class AI needs to be backed up by intuitive UI to be truly impactful to our customers. 
   
   - You will own large parts of the customer experience that will ultimately help our customers protect their organizations from attackers.

 - Help create a robust engineering culture at Cogent
   
   - Onboard, support and uplevel future team members
   
   - Participate in, provide feedback on, and iterate interview process as we scale the team up
   
   - Actively contribute to Engineering Excellence, Operational Excellence and Recruiting initiatives




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of professional experience as a hands-on engineer, working across the full software stack and leading the development of scalable, high-quality applications

 - Expertise in designing, developing, and maintaining backend systems, including microservices, APIs, and databases, to support modern web applications

 - Strong experience with frontend development, including modern JavaScript frameworks (React, Vue, Angular) and web technologies (HTML, CSS, TypeScript)

 - Ability to work across the stack, integrating backend services with dynamic, user-facing frontends to deliver seamless, performant product experiences

 - Proficient in API design (RESTful, GraphQL, or gRPC) and integrating with both internal and external systems

 - Familiarity with containerization and orchestration technologies (Docker, Kubernetes) and deployment pipelines (CI/CD) in cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure)

 - Solid experience in Agile development processes, including code reviews, sprint planning, and collaborative cross-functional team work to deliver full-stack product features




FOR CALIFORNIA BASED APPLICANTS

The standard base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $3

source_jobs.title: Data Platform Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent Security is on a mission to stop breaches and prevent cybercrime by innovating at the frontier of generative AI systems. We are building the world’s first AI cyber taskforce, composed of AI agents capable of human-caliber reasoning and execution of cybersecurity tasks, that autonomously protects organizations from emerging threats. The early adopters of our technology include some of the world’s most important institutions, spanning public companies, elite universities, and Fortune 500 corporations across industries. 



Cogent was founded by a seasoned team of former engineering and product leaders, who bring decades of experience across cybersecurity and technology. The early team is fully in-person in San Francisco and New York, and consists of the top software engineering and machine learning talent from leading companies such as Abnormal Security, Coinbase, Microsoft, Tesla, Stripe and more. To support our ambitious growth plans, we recently raised a large Seed round from top investors and angels across AI, cybersecurity, and enterprise software. 



As we execute on our mission, we are constantly pushing ourselves to ACHIEVe:

 - Ambition for Excellence 
   
   - We work backwards from the way things should be and constantly measure our progress against it

 - Customer Centricity 
   
   - We obsess over the problems our customers face and relentlessly innovate to find the best solutions

 - Intellectual Honesty 
   
   - We embrace hard conversations and actively seek the truth

 - Intentionality 
   
   - We exhibit good judgment and are thoughtful about tradeoffs

 - Extreme Ownership 
   
   - We take pride in our work and never say the words “not my problem”

 - Velocity / Bias for Action
   
   - We don’t leave for tomorrow what can be done today



As we extend our founding team, we are looking for talented, ambitious individuals who are excited to build in the Applied AI space!




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for Founding- and Staff-level Engineers to design and implement the foundational pillars of Cogent’s data platform and integration pipeline in order to reliably ingest and normalize enterprise data sources across many corporate and IT systems and then to enable the transformation of that data into a knowledge base that can be leveraged by other GenAI systems.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Build the new standard for ingesting and extracting enterprise data
   
   - Your role will involve building data connectors, running data pipelines, and extracting insights and context from our customer’s enterprise environments. 
   
   - You should get excited about building robust and secure backend systems that handle large volumes of data, ETLs, as well as reimagining how enterprise security teams should extract, store and access data.

 - Design highly performant data pipelines and indexing strategies that enable Applied AI use cases like semantic search and retrieval augmented generation
   
   - Work with Applied AI teams to taxonomize, correlate and deduplicate data across various enterprise sources, and architect indexing, storage and query strategies for data in Cogent’s data lake.

 - Architect the modern data platform for Gen AI
   
   - The LLM stack and Gen AI landscape is changing everyday. You’ll be front and center of making build vs buy decisions for various tools and services needed by the data platform, and integrating these decisions into our broader architecture and tech roadmap. 
   
   - Implement generalizable design patterns and system components that allow us to scale from 1 integration to thousands of integrations




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of professional experience as a hands-on engineer and technical leader leading multiple projects

 - Experience building data pipelines for Information Retrieval Systems (such as knowledge graphs, search engines, or similar) for enterprise use cases

 - Experience in ETL orchestration and workflow management tools

source_jobs.title: Agent Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Agent Engineer at Cogent Security, you will design, build, and deploy mission-critical AI agents that operate in complex customer environments and drive measurable security outcomes. This is a highly cross-functional, forward-deployed role — you’ll work directly with customers to understand their needs, adapt our platform to their environment, and continuously iterate on solutions that scale to millions of real-world events.

You’ll own projects end-to-end: from initial implementation and data onboarding, to deploying in production, to integrating feedback into our core agent platform. Your work will directly shape how AI agents detect threats, triage incidents, and automate security workflows for some of the world’s most sophisticated organizations.

This role is ideal for engineers who thrive in high-impact environments, love solving hard technical problems, and want to see the tangible results of their work in the field.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design & Deploy AI Agents — Build security-focused AI agents that can reason over large datasets, interact with security systems, and reliably handle complex real-world workflows.
   

 - Own Implementations End-to-End — Lead technical delivery for new and existing customers, from data ingestion and transformation pipelines to configuration, workflow design, and feature extension.
   

 - Integrate the Latest Models at Scale — Experiment with, evaluate, and integrate cutting-edge text, voice, and multi-modal models into production environments.
   

 - Adapt to Diverse Environments — Work directly with customer security teams to integrate agents with their tools, processes, and data sources.
   

 - Drive Platform Evolution — Identify cross-customer trends and translate field insights into platform improvements and new capabilities.
   

 - Solve Complex Technical Problems — Debug deep in the stack, investigate edge cases, and ensure our agents meet enterprise-grade reliability and performance standards.
   






ABOUT YOU

 - 2+ years of professional software engineering experience, ideally in high-velocity or customer-facing environments.
   

 - Proficiency in Python and TypeScript, with strong skills in asynchronous programming and building robust data integrations.
   

 - Strong debugging and systems thinking abilities; comfortable tracing issues across multiple layers of a stack.
   

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate between technical and non-technical contexts.
   

 - Thrive in ambiguity, take ownership end-to-end, and prioritize customer outcomes over output.
   

Bonus Points For:

 - Experience with multi-modal AI models, enterprise SaaS, security operations, or behavioral analytics.
   

 - Familiarity with large-scale data ingest

source_jobs.title: Security Lead (Vulnerability Management) - SF/NYC/Remote (US)
source_jobs.description:
Cogent Security is on a mission to stop breaches and prevent cybercrime by innovating at the frontier of generative AI systems. We are building the world’s first AI cyber taskforce, composed of AI agents capable of human-caliber reasoning and execution of cybersecurity tasks, that autonomously protects organizations from emerging threats. The early adopters of our technology include some of the world’s most important institutions, spanning public companies, elite universities, and Fortune 500 corporations across industries.



Cogent was founded by a seasoned team of former engineering and product leaders, who bring decades of experience across cybersecurity and technology. The team is fully in-person in San Francisco and New York, and consists of the top software engineering and machine learning talent from leading companies such as Abnormal Security, Coinbase, Microsoft, Tesla, Stripe and more. To support our ambitious growth plans, we recently raised a large Seed round led by Greylock Partners and leading angels across AI, cybersecurity, and enterprise software (e.g. Reid Hoffman and founders of Abnormal, Datadog, and other top companies).



As we execute on our mission, we are constantly pushing ourselves to ACHIEVe:

 - Ambition for Excellence
   
   - We work backwards from the way things should be and constantly measure our progress against it

 - Customer Centricity
   
   - We obsess over the problems our customers face and relentlessly innovate to find the best solutions

 - Intellectual Honesty
   
   - We embrace hard conversations and actively seek the truth

 - Intentionality
   
   - We exhibit good judgment and are thoughtful about tradeoffs

 - Extreme Ownership
   
   - We take pride in our work and never say the words “not my problem”

 - Velocity / Bias for Action
   
   - We don’t leave for tomorrow what can be done today




ABOUT THE ROLE

As Security Lead, you will be both a key architect of our product and a steward of Cogent’s own operational security.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Shape the Cogent product at the frontier of AI and cybersecurity
   
   - Work hand-in-hand with machine learning engineers to build AI agents grounded in real-world security workflows
   
   - Contribute deep domain insight to shape product strategy, roadmap, and core capabilities

 - Build the world’s first AI-native cybersecurity Org
   
   - Extend and evolve Cogent’s security posture, systems, and incident response capabilities to create the strongest cyber program in the industry
   
   - Implement processes and tools to protect Cogent and our customers end to end

 - Educate the market and elevate the industry
   
   - Write thought-provoking content, partner with customers, and speak credibly about the role of AI in vulnerability management
   
   - Help define how the industry thinks about autonomous security




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

You are a top 1% builder who thrives at the intersection of security and engineering. You bring:

 - 10+ years of deep, hands-on experience in security engineering

 - Expertise in vulnerability management across the attack surface

 - Fluency in Python or a comparable language, with a passion for automation

 - A track record of operating at scale in high-stakes, fast-paced environments

 - A bias for clarity, velocity, and technical rigor

 - (Bonus) Experience working closely with ML, AI, or data science teams




FOR CALIFORNIA BASED APPLICANTS

The standard base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $300,000 annually. Compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity, and benefits.

We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse company. We do not discriminate based on gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, civil or family status, age, disability, or race.

source_jobs.title: Staff Frontend Engineer (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Staff Frontend Engineer, you will own the frontend platform and experience that enables customers to visualize, understand, and act on complex security data with confidence.



This is a Staff+ caliber role: you’ve operated at least at Staff, Senior Staff, or Principal Frontend Engineer level at some point in your career, and you’re excited to set the technical direction for a brand-new platform. You will design the frontend infrastructure, build “golden” component libraries, and make the platform AI-friendly, all while continuing to ship customer-facing features.



You’ll combine deep architectural thinking with hands-on development, ensuring that our frontend stack is both elegant and resilient as we scale. Over time, you’ll help mentor other frontend engineers and we will build a team around the foundations you create.



What You’ll Do

 - Define Frontend Architecture – Establish the core architecture and patterns for our web applications, laying the foundation for long-term scalability and performance.

 - Build Golden Libraries & Platform – Create reusable component libraries and tooling that set the standard for quality and enable rapid, consistent development across the team.

 - Build AI-Driven, User-Focused Features – While setting up core frontend infrastructure, design and deliver high-impact features that seamlessly integrate AI-powered capabilities, ensuring clarity, usability, and reliability for complex security workflows.

 - Mentor and Influence – Serve as a mentor and technical leader for future frontend engineers; help us grow a team that values craftsmanship and excellence.

 - Drive Engineering Craftsmanship – Uphold high standards of code quality, attention to detail, and thoughtful design. Balance speed with sustainable engineering practices.



About You

 - Have operated at Staff+ level (Staff, Senior Staff, or Principal Frontend Engineer) at some point in your career.

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience

 - Deep expertise in React, TypeScript, and modern web application architecture.

 - Strong background in frontend infrastructure—component libraries, build systems, state management, and performance optimization.

 - Demonstrated ability to work autonomously, owning projects end-to-end with minimal oversight

 - Exceptional attention to detail and a passion for engineering craftsmanship.

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills; thrive in fast-paced, high-impact environments.



Why Cogent Security

Cogent Security is redefining how AI can proactively defend organizations against evolving threats. Our mission is to empower security teams with cutting-edge AI agents and clear, actionable insights. You’ll join a high-slope engineering team where your work di

source_jobs.title: Regional Sales Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent ML research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Regional Sales Manager, you will be responsible for breaking into and closing enterprise deals across a large territory. You will manage complex (and sometimes long) sales cycles with high ACVs and introduce Cogent as a preferred AI transformation partner across vulnerability management and broader cybersecurity.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Drive net-new enterprise logo acquisition as well as expansion within existing strategic accounts.

 - Lead complex sales cycles involving CISOs, CIOs, and other executive stakeholders.

 - Work closely with strategic channel partners to accelerate enterprise traction

 - Attend industry events and build the company brand




ABOUT YOU

 - 7+ years of enterprise sales experience in cybersecurity

 - Proven track record of closing large six and seven figure enterprise deals with Global 2000 organizations

 - Experience selling technical products across multiple stakeholders

 - Deep industry relationships across customers, partners, and vendors

 - Able to deal with ambiguity

 - Strong “hunter” with great communication skills and presence




WHY COGENT

Cogent is redefining how AI can proactively defend organizations against evolving threats. Our mission is to empower security teams with cutting-edge AI agents and clear, actionable insights. You’ll join a world-class GTM team where your work directly impacts the safety of some of the world’s most sophisticated organizations.

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.

To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.

Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.

We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent ML research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent is building a world-class marketing team, and we're looking for smart, fast, exceptional people who are committed to our mission and bring a spark of creativity that sets us apart from traditional B2B marketing. As a founding member of product marketing, you'll have an immediate, highly visible impact on how Cogent shows up in a crowded market and defines an entirely new category. If you like crafting content that is clear, specific, and actually resonates with security practitioners (without the typical "marketing speak"), you'll fit in well here. You'll own Cogent's messaging, positioning, and go-to-market enablement. You're the connective tissue between Product, Sales, Customer Success, and the rest of Marketing. Your job is to translate real product differentiation into stories, proof, and sales tools that win enterprise deals.





WHAT YOU'LL DO



Messaging & Positioning

 - Define and evolve Cogent's core narrative: category, differentiation, "why now," and "why us."

 - Build segmentation and ICP clarity and tailor messaging by persona and use case.

 - Own competitive positioning, battlecards, and win/loss insights.



Sales Enablement

 - Create and maintain the core sales deck(s), demo storyline inputs, one-pagers, and solution briefs.

 - Build enablement that improves conversion at each stage: discovery, demo, POC, security review, close.

 - Partner with the sales team on objection handling, pricing/packaging narratives, and proof points.



Launches & Product Communication

 - Own product and feature launch strategy, both internal readiness and external storytelling.

 - Write product web pages and solution pages that are crisp, credible, and conversion-aware.

 - Build content that supports evaluation: evaluation guides, operationalization docs, ROI/value frameworks.



Customer Proof

 - Turn customer outcomes into proof: case studies, quantified value, reference-ready narratives.

 - Partner with CS on customer councils, reference loops, and voice-of-customer insights.



AI-Native Execution

 - Use AI to accelerate drafts, variants, and repurposing while maintaining high standards, and accuracy.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Must-Have

 - 7-10+ years in B2B product marketing, with a strong cybersecurity background.

 - Deep familiarity with enterprise security buyers and workflows.

 - Proven ability to create positioning, sales decks, web copy, enablement, competitive assets, and launch plans.

 - Excellent writing, taste, and clarity. You can explain complex security concepts simply and credibly.



Nice-to-Have

 - Experience in vulnerability management, exposure management, ASM/CAASM, AppSec/CloudSec adjacencies.

 - Experience with channel partners, co-sell motions, and partner enablement.

 - Exp

 - Preeminent ML research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent is building a world-class marketing team, and we're looking for smart, fast, exceptional people who are committed to our mission and bring a spark of creativity that sets us apart from traditional B2B marketing. As an early marketing hire, you'll have an immediate, highly visible impact. If you're a creator at heart who also knows how to build systems, and you're tired of scheduling posts for brands that have nothing to say, keep reading. This is not a "post scheduler" role. You'll be a creator-operator who can take internal work (product insights, research, customer wins, point of view) and turn it into content that travels. You'll partner closely with the VP of Marketing, Product Marketing, and technical leaders to build a consistent presence, and selectively through creator/influencer collaborations.




WHAT YOU'LL DO



Organic Social That Drives Real Attention

 - Own Cogent's organic social strategy and execution (primary: LinkedIn; secondary: X/Reddit/Instagram/TikTok as appropriate).

 - Create a weekly content cadence across POV, product value, customer proof, research packaging, and culture/talent content.

 - Build "content series" that compound over time (e.g., weekly VM insights, AI security myths, remediation playbooks).



Executive/Founder Voice

 - Ghostwrite for CEO, CTO, and key leaders with a strong point of view and consistent tone.

 - Build repeatable systems for capturing raw material: interviews, Slack mining, call notes, research briefs.



Content Production

 - Produce and edit content: short videos, motion-lite explainers, quote cards, carousels, blog-to-social repurposing.

 - Use AI tools to accelerate ideation, drafting, editing, and versioning while keeping quality and credibility high.



Influencers & Collaboration

 - Manage paid influencer/creator relationships (selectively and tastefully) aligned to enterprise security audiences.

 - Build partnerships with practitioners and communities for content collaborations: podcasts, AMAs, guest posts.



Story Packaging & Internal Project Management

 - Turn threat research, AI research, or product insights into marketable stories with clear narratives and guardrails.

 - Coordinate with PMM on accuracy, claims, and launch timing.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Must-Have

 - 2-5+ years in social/content roles for B2B tech (cybersecurity preferred).

 - Proven track record of growing organic presence and engagement with an enterprise audience.

 - Excellent writing skills and a sharp editorial eye. You can develop a point of view without fluff.

 - Strong production ability: you can create visually polished assets yourself or manage contractors who do.

 - Comfortable working with technical stakeholders and translating complex topics into compe

source_jobs.title: Technical Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY 

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.



To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.



Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.



We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL
   
   


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Technical Account Manager focused on customer deployment, you'll own the technical success of new customers from kickoff through initial production rollout and early expansion. You'll be the person customers call when they need to understand how Cogent fits into their stack, unblock an integration, or get executive buy-in for a broader rollout. You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Support to make sure deployments land on time, configurations match real-world needs, and customers see measurable value fast.

This is a hands-on, customer-facing role for someone who enjoys the mix of technical depth, project ownership, and relationship building — and who knows that a great deployment experience is the foundation of a long-term account.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Own the end-to-end deployment journey for new customers, from technical kickoff through go-live and early adoption milestones.

 - Build and maintain customer-specific deployment plans, including timelines, success criteria, integration requirements, and risk mitigation.

 - Serve as the primary technical point of contact during deployment, translating between customer engineering teams and Cogent's Product and Engineering organizations.

 - Lead architecture and integration discussions, advising customers on best practices for scale, security, and reliability in their environment.

 - Troubleshoot complex technical issues during rollout — debugging configurations, API integrations, authentication flows, and data pipelines — and drive them to resolution.

 - Develop reusable playbooks, runbooks, and deployment artifacts that help every future customer get to value faster.

 - Partner with Sales and Customer Success to identify expansion opportunities surfaced during deployment and hand off accounts cleanly once customers are in steady state.

 - Advocate for customers internally by surfacing product gaps, documentation needs, and deployment friction to Product and Engineering.

 - Contribute to internal tooling and process improvements that scale the deployment function as Cogent grows.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of experience in a technical, customer-facing role — Technical Account Manager, Solutions Engineer, Deployment Engineer, Implementation Consultant, or similar.

 - Proven track record leading technical deployments or implementations for enterprise or mid-market B2B SaaS customers.

 - Strong technical foundation: comfortable reading and discussing code, reasoning about APIs and authentication, working in the command line, and navigating cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure).

 - Familiarity with modern data and integration patterns — REST and webhooks, SSO/SAML/OIDC, containerization, CI/CD concepts.

 - Excel

source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations (RevOps)
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

About Cogent Security

Cogent is redefining vulnerability management with an AI-native platform that helps security teams cut through the noise and fix what matters. We're backed by top-tier investors, growing rapidly, and building the go-to-market engine to match.

About the Role

Cogent is a premier early-stage AI startup, and we've built one of the most differentiated GTM motions in cybersecurity. The growth function runs on an in-house AI operating system: a rich data layer and multi-agent platform that handles revenue marketing, sales ops, field logistics, and pipeline operations. Two people doing the output of ten. This role joins a small team reshaping how a security company goes to market.

RevOps is the analytical engine of the GTM team. Every decision we make is rooted in data: how the funnel converts at each stage, by source, by rep, on what time frame. You'll diagnose where the funnel breaks, redesign the processes to fix it, and partner with sales leadership on the follow-through. The dashboards and agents already exist. We need someone who can interpret what they're showing and turn the so what into outcomes.

This is the right role if you came up in investment banking, consulting, or strategy and moved into a tech GTM function. You don't need to know cyber. You do need advanced analytical skills, because every decision at Cogent is rooted in data. That's my background too. I built quant-rooted GTM strategies at prior companies that drove very fast growth, and I want to hire someone who brings the same lens.

What You'll Do

Pipeline and deal operations

 - Right hand to the Head of Growth across the operating rhythm: pod calls, forecast calls, pipe jams. Surface what needs action and shepherd it through to follow-through

 - Pipeline hygiene: define when opportunities should be created versus when accounts belong on a target list, design the staging discipline, and keep the pipeline data trustworthy

 - Conversion analysis across the full funnel: how things convert at each stage, on what time frame, by source, by rep. Diagnose where deals stall, classify why, and partner with sales leadership on improving the rates

 - Maintain target account lists per rep per territory and translate the ICP into targeting reps and partners can actually use

 - Weekly closed-loss and DQ analysis. Look for DQ'd pipeline worth reactivating when product or market conditions change

Forecasting and revenue planning

 - Run forecast operations so the forecast call becomes a decision-making tool, not a status update. Track actuals against targets and surface risk early

 - Use deal scoring signals to prioritize where leadership spends time and which deals need intervention

 - Capacity planning as the team scales: rep productivity, POV prioritization, territory balancing, and resource allocation

Sales process and enablement

 - Per-rep performance analysis: deal velocity, conversion rates, activity patterns, win/loss trends. Surface what works and help close the gap

 - Design the sales process alongside sales leadership: opportunity criteria, stage gating, required fields, DQ reasons that feed back to product

 - Help close the enablement gap. Tools and automation exist but reps don't always use them. Make the right behavior the easy one

Special projects

 - Partner strategy: spiff programs, channel investment decisions, and working with the Director of Strategic Partnerships on where to double down based on performance data

 - Build business cases for new GTM motions: risk assessments, no-POV deals, expansion plays

 - Every week looks a little different. The role flexes with what the business needs

What We're Looking For

 - 3-6 years in revenue operations, sales operations, strategy, or GTM operations at a B2B SaaS company. Cybersecurity experience is not a requirement

 - Banking or consulting background that pivoted into tech GTM is highly preferred. You need to be extremely quantitative and analytical: comf

source_jobs.title: Cogent AI Fellowship (Member of Technical Staff)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.

 

To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.

 

Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.

 

We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent Research is investing equally in two core research areas. The first is agentic security: as AI agents are deployed in production, real-world incidents have shown that prompt-level guardrails are insufficient. Agent behavior requires guarantees that are non-bypassable, AI-synthesized, and human-auditable. The second is auto-remediation: resolving security findings at machine speed in real customer environments.

Each area is a first-class research objective, and the two are deeply connected. Auto-remediation can only operate safely at the required level of autonomy when agentic security guarantees are in place. Across both areas, we combine AI with formal methods: tool calls governed by runtime-enforced policies, and code fixes that ship with machine-checkable certificates of correctness.

The Cogent AI Fellowship is an early-career research-engineering track for recently graduated, or soon-to-graduate, PhDs who want to move research from paper to production. Fellows choose a problem they are excited to pursue, prototype quickly alongside our research and engineering teams, and ship production systems. We look for people who are comfortable with ambiguity and willing to change direction when the evidence calls for it.

 
 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Define and pursue a research direction at the intersection of AI and formal methods, such as autoformalizing intent into machine-checkable specifications, certificate-producing code generation, AI-assisted proof in Lean, runtime verification of agent tool calls, or natural-language-to-policy synthesis.

 - Take ideas end-to-end: from prototype to systems running against real customer workloads.

 - Partner with applied AI, agent, and platform engineers to translate research advances into production capabilities.

 - Work with design partners and security leaders to ground the work in the workflows security engineers actually run.

 - Build evaluation harnesses and benchmarks that capture what “verified-correct” or “policy-compliant” means in messy, real-world security environments.

 - Iterate quickly, update your approach when results indicate a better path, and share what you learn internally and at venues that advance the field.

 
 


ABOUT YOU

 - Research experience in one or more of the following areas: formal methods, program analysis, programming languages, agentic AI for code reasoning, or runtime verification.

 - A strong publication record in top conferences or journals, or comparable open-source or artifact contributions.

 - A recently completed PhD in Computer Science, or a closely related field, or a defense expected soon.

 - Strong engineering ability, including fluency in a major programming language and the judgment to mature a prototype into a production-

source_jobs.title: AI Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.

 

To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.

 

Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.

 

We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent Research is the applied AI lab at the heart of Cogent's mission. As an AI Research Engineer, you will work at the frontier of automated reasoning and AI-driven code analysis — inventing novel solutions, prototyping fast, and pivoting when first ideas don't pan out.

This is not a role for those who need a playbook. We are looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, drives work proactively and independently, and is deeply aligned with Cogent's mission to make the world's infrastructure safer.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Conduct original research at the intersection of AI and automated code reasoning, advancing the state of the art in areas such as static/dynamic analysis, formal verification, and automated testing

 - Invent and apply novel AI techniques to challenges in code reasoning and code generation, from early hypothesis through working prototype

 - Design and execute automated testing and fuzzing strategies to validate and stress-test systems under development

 - Navigate ambiguity with a proactive mindset — adapt quickly when initial approaches don't pan out and iterate toward robust solutions

 - Publish research findings at top-tier conferences and peer-reviewed journals, contributing to the broader scientific community

 - Collaborate with engineering and product teams to translate research breakthroughs into Cogent's core AI platform

 - Stay on top of cutting-edge developments in ML and program analysis, and propose incorporating them into Cogent's systems where appropriate




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Experience in Automated Reasoning: Hands-on experience with static/dynamic analysis, formal verification, and/or automated testing and fuzzing

 - AI Expertise: A track record of developing or applying AI/ML techniques to code reasoning or code generation problems

 - Research Publication: Demonstrated research output — papers accepted at recognized conferences or peer-reviewed journals

 - Education: Degree in Computer Science or a closely related technical field (BS, MS, or PhD)

 - Independence: A highly self-directed, proactive, and self-starting attitude — you identify problems, propose solutions, and execute without being told

 - Mission Alignment: A genuine passion for Cogent's mission and a belief that this work matters

Bonus Points For:

 - Experience in cybersecurity — knowledge of vulnerability classes, exploit techniques, or secure-by-design systems

 - Familiarity with Cloud Infrastructure as Code (IaC) — tools like Terraform, Pulumi, CDK, or equivalent




FOR CALIFORNIA BASED APPLICANTS

The standard base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $300,000 annually. Compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and e

source_jobs.title: Research Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent’s "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.

 

To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.

 

Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.

 

We’re backed by Greylock and we’ve built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

Cogent Research is the applied AI lab at the heart of Cogent's mission. As a Research Scientist, you will drive foundational and applied research at the frontier of automated reasoning and AI-driven code analysis — producing work that is both scientifically rigorous and directly impactful in the real world.

We are looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, invents novel solutions when existing ones fall short, and has the flexibility to change course when initial ideas don't pan out. If you have deep expertise in automated reasoning, a strong publication or artifact track record, and a passion for making the world's infrastructure safer, we want to hear from you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO & ACHIEVE

 - Drive original, high-impact research in automated reasoning, code analysis, and AI-driven security, from early hypothesis through publication-quality results

 - Invent and apply novel AI techniques to challenges in code reasoning and code generation, advancing the state of the art

 - Develop significant research artifacts, tools, or systems that have real-world impact inside Cogent and in the broader research community

 - Design and execute automated testing and fuzzing strategies to validate systems under development

 - Publish findings at top-tier conferences and peer-reviewed journals

 - Collaborate with engineering and product teams to translate research into Cogent's core AI platform

 - Stay at the cutting edge of developments in ML, program analysis, and formal methods — and help shape Cogent's research direction
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Extensive Experience in Automated Reasoning: Deep hands-on expertise in static/dynamic analysis, formal verification, and/or automated testing and fuzzing

 - AI Expertise: Proven experience developing or applying AI/ML techniques to code reasoning or code generation problems

 - Education: An MSc or PhD/DPhil in Computer Science or a closely related technical field

 - Proven Track Record: Demonstrated impact through publications at top-tier conferences, significant artifacts developed in a professional setting, or widely-adopted open-source contributions

 - Independence: A highly self-directed, proactive, and self-starting attitude — you identify problems, propose solutions, and drive them to completion

 - Mission Alignment: A genuine passion for Cogent's mission and a belief that this work matters

Bonus Points For:

 - Experience in cybersecurity — knowledge of vulnerability classes, exploit techniques, or secure-by-design systems

 - Familiarity with Cloud Infrastructure as Code (IaC) — tools like Terraform, Pulumi, CDK, or equivalent




FOR CALIFORNIA BASED APPLICANTS

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer (US West)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COGENT SECURITY

Cogent is an Applied AI Lab building the next generation of AI agents for cybersecurity. AI has fundamentally changed how attacks happen, allowing malicious actors to operate at unprecedented speed and scale. Cogent's "AI Taskforce" assesses petabytes of enterprise data to remediate these issues before critical breaches occur.

To stay at the cutting edge, we blend frontier research with real-world execution. Alongside our core product work, Cogent Research serves as our applied AI lab, providing the research horsepower needed to make truly agentic security workflows a reality.

Since coming out of stealth, Cogent has experienced rapid growth. We partner with Fortune 500 companies to secure some of the most complex production environments in the world.

We're backed by Greylock and we've built a team with the best minds in applied AI. Our team is comprised of people from:

 - Top universities like Stanford, Berkeley, Penn, Duke, Carnegie Mellon, Waterloo

 - Unicorn, high-growth companies like Scale AI, Databricks, Stripe, Tesla, Coinbase

 - World class cybersecurity experts from Wiz, Abnormal AI, Zscaler

 - Preeminent research labs like Deepmind and SAIL




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Solutions Engineer at Cogent Security, you'll be the technical backbone of our post-sales motion - designing and scoping the vulnerability management solutions that get our most complex customers live on Cogent. You're not writing production code, but you're technical enough to speak the same language as a customer's security engineers, translate their environment into a concrete implementation plan, and write the tickets that turn that plan into reality.



This role sits within Customer Success, working alongside TAMs who own the relationship and success metrics, while you own the technical design. Over time, we want to reduce how much of this work requires a dedicated Forward Deployed Engineer for every implementation - which means the person in this seat needs deep enough domain expertise in vulnerability management to scope things right the first time.



This is a founding role for someone who's comfortable with ambiguity, has done this before at an early-stage company, and has sat across the table from CISOs and enterprise security teams.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Scope & Design Solutions - Work directly with customer security teams to understand their environment and design how their Cogent instance should be built and deployed.

 - Bridge Technical & Product - Write clear, actionable tickets that hand off your solution design to engineering for build-out.

 - Own the Technical Relationship - Serve as the technical counterpart to the TAM, speaking the same language as customer security engineers and earning credibility with technical stakeholders up to CISO level.

 - Deploy at Enterprise Scale - Get customers live on Cogent, working with deployment teams that can span dozens of stakeholders on the customer side.

 - Scale our Forward Deployed Motion - Build repeatable scoping and design practices that shrink how much forward-deployed engineering is needed per implementation.

 - Shape What "Good" Looks Like - As an early hire on this team, help define the Solutions Architect function itself as Cogent's post-sales motion scales.




ABOUT YOU

 - 10+ years of professional experience, including 5+ years of deep domain expertise in vulnerability management or a closely adjacent security discipline.

 - Experience at an early-stage company - you've helped scale and build out processes from scratch, and you're comfortable operating with ambiguity rather than a fully-defined playbook.

 - A track record with enterprise-scale customers - you've worked with CISO/exec-level stakeholders and large deployment teams (e.g., 30-person implementations).

 - Management experience, including having hired and built out a team.

 - Familiarity with APIs - comfortable with API keys, reading/writing data, and validating data in a tool, even

notes: Highly technical job postings with a focus on AI and cybersecurity. Strong research component.
